your_book:
Thursday was a rough session for Jaleel's book, with the portfolio sitting at $20,714 and a cumulative unrealized loss of roughly $1,044 (−4.8% overall). The damage today was concentrated in software: Adobe dropped 2.74% to $3,327 and ServiceNow fell 3.59% to $2,117, together accounting for more than a quarter of the portfolio — and both sold off on the same headline, reports that OpenAI is building an AI-agent platform called Presence that could undercut legacy software workflows. That's a narrative hit, not an earnings miss, but with ADBE already down 13.4% from cost and NOW down 3.4%, the market is clearly not waiting for proof. Danaher was the bright spot, surging 7.04% to $1,342 — a meaningful single-session gain that partially offset the software drag.

The drift problem that deserves scrutiny is ADBE specifically: rated Strong Buy with a QARP of 75.2, yet down 13.4% from cost and now facing a compounding narrative headwind (AI-moat fear, CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis) layered on top of today's OpenAI news. Price and thesis are visibly diverging. RKLB is the other long-running mismatch — down 23.6% from cost, carrying an AVOID verdict and a QARP of just 43.5, yet still holds 9.5% of the book. BSX, flagged for a guidance slash, reports July 29 — six days away.

Watch the software cluster into next week: if the OpenAI Presence story gains traction, ADBE and NOW face further multiple compression regardless of fundamentals. BSX's July 29 print is the nearest hard catalyst. RKLB's position size relative to its AVOID rating remains the structural issue that hasn't been resolved.

attention.headline: Largest AVOID in the book, down 23.6% — position size still unexplained
attention.note: RKLB is 9.5% of the portfolio with an AVOID verdict and a QARP of 43.5; at a $1,958 value it is the fifth-largest holding, and no data provided justifies that weight against a capital-preservation mandate.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings print July 29 — six days away
attention.note: Boston Scientific already cut FY26 growth guidance to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3; the next print on July 29 will confirm or deepen that trajectory with limited time to act beforehand.

attention.headline: Down 13.4% from cost and hit again today on OpenAI Presence news
attention.note: ADBE fell another 2.74% Thursday as the OpenAI AI-agent story erodes confidence in legacy software moats — layered on top of the existing CFO exit and ARR de-emphasis flags; at 16.1% of the book, the gap between the Strong Buy thesis and the price action is widening and needs reassessment.

attention.headline: Largest single holding dropped 3.59% on same AI-disruption headline as ADBE
attention.note: ServiceNow at 10.2% weight fell sharply on the OpenAI Presence report; with a high multiple, no upcoming near-term catalyst (next report October 27), and macro-rate sensitivity already flagged, the position is exposed to further de-rating if the narrative sticks.
